message:
fix: search interference, update errors, MCP install, health indicators, single-cluster (#4582)

- Use deduplicatedClusters in Pods.tsx and AlertRuleEditor.tsx to avoid
  double-counting when multiple kubeconfig contexts point to the same
  physical cluster (#4513)
- Clear global searchQuery when user types in tab-specific search fields
  in MissionBrowser; add "filtered by global search" indicator (#4559)
- Show "Showing X of Y missions" count header in mission browser
  recommended tab regardless of active filters (#4560)
- Display actual error message inline in UpdateSettings with retry
  guidance instead of generic "Error checking updates" (#4555)
- Add brew install command for kubestellar-ops and kubestellar-deploy
  in start.sh MCP binary warning (#4554)
- Add cluster health summary (connected/disconnected) to
  GPUDashboardTab (#4515)

Fixes #4513, #4515, #4554, #4555, #4559, #4560

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -332,7 +332,11 @@ if [ -n "$MCP_MISSING" ]; then
     echo ""
     echo "  Note: $MCP_MISSING not found on PATH."
     echo "  MCP tools (Kubernetes ops and deploy) will be unavailable."
-    echo "  To install, follow Step 1 of the Quick Start guide:"
+    echo ""
+    echo "  Quick install via Homebrew:"
+    echo "    brew install kubestellar/tap/kubestellar-ops kubestellar/tap/kubestellar-deploy"
+    echo ""
+    echo "  Or follow the full Quick Start guide:"
     echo "    https://kubestellar.io/docs/console/overview/quick-start#step-1-install-kubestellar-mcp-tools"
     echo ""
 fi

@@ -676,7 +676,15 @@ export function UpdateSettings() { {formatLastChecked()} - {error &&

{error}

} + {error && ( +

{t('settings.updates.errorChecking')}
